subject: Math
badgeName: Measurement Conversion, Volume, and Data Badge

game_theme.title: Measurement Conversion, Volume, and Data
game_theme.mission_intro: Use convert measurements, find volume, and interpret fractional line plots to solve real Grade 5 math missions.
game_theme.badge_name: Measurement Conversion, Volume, and Data Badge

lesson.objective: convert measurements, find volume, and interpret fractional line plots
lesson.concept_explanation: convert measurements, find volume, and interpret fractional line plots means reading the situation, matching it to a visual model, and using precise Grade 5 math language before answering.
lesson.common_misconception: A common mistake is focusing on one number or one visible feature without checking the labels, units, axes, or attributes that give the problem meaning.
lesson.real_world_connection: Students, scientists, designers, builders, and data readers use these skills to describe and solve real problems clearly.
lesson.lesson_summary: Use the model, name the important labels, and answer with accurate conversions, cubic units, volume, length, width, height, line plots, fractional measurements.

page_audio.text: This mini lesson introduces convert measurements, find volume, and interpret fractional line plots. The visual model is part of the thinking, not decoration. Say the important labels aloud, then solve with the correct math language.
page_audio.label: Read This Page

worked_examples.title: Convert meters to centimeters
worked_examples.text: Three meters equals three groups of one hundred centimeters, so multiply 3 by 100 to get 300 centimeters.

page_audio.text: When converting meters to centimeters, move from a larger unit to a smaller unit. That means one meter becomes one hundred centimeters. Three meters becomes three hundred centimeters.
page_audio.label: Read This Page

worked_examples.title: Volume with length, width, and height
worked_examples.text: A prism that is 4 units long, 3 units wide, and 2 units high has 4 × 3 × 2, or 24 cubic units.

page_audio.text: Volume counts cubic units that fill a solid. First find one layer with length times width. Then multiply by height to count every layer.
page_audio.label: Read This Page

story.text: Welcome to Measurement Conversion, Volume, and Data. In this mission, you will use convert measurements, find volume, and interpret fractional line plots to solve problems that feel like real classroom and world tasks. Watch how the labels, units, and visual model tell you what to do next.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: This lesson teaches convert measurements, find volume, and interpret fractional line plots. Read the model before calculating, because the model shows what each number or attribute means. Use precise math language: conversions, cubic units, volume, length, width, height, line plots, fractional measurements.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: On the Watch page, slow down and follow one complete example. First name what the question asks, then connect each label in the visual to the operation or property. Finally, say the answer in a complete math sentence.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: On the Demo page, build the reasoning with support. Notice where common mistakes happen, such as reversing coordinates, converting the wrong direction, or using only one shape attribute. The model is here to help you catch the mistake early.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: This Practice page lets you try the skill yourself. Tap Read Question before you answer so the measurement, coordinate, or geometry words are spoken clearly. Then use the visual and choose or type the answer carefully.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: This Challenge page may combine ideas or ask for a deeper explanation. Tap Read Question, study the visual, and decide which information matters most. If the answer has a unit, coordinate, or shape category, include it.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: This Checkpoint page checks what you can do independently. Tap Read Question, solve without rushing, and compare your answer to the model. Your response should show that you understand the convert measurements, find volume, and interpret fractional line plots.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: You reached the badge page for Measurement Conversion, Volume, and Data. Think about the model that helped most and the misconception you avoided. This badge celebrates careful reasoning, not just a quick answer.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: The profile page saves your growth for Measurement Conversion, Volume, and Data. Review your strengths, then continue to the Skill Practice Center for focused levels or Mixed review. Choose Practice This Skill when you want more questions with Read Question support.
profile.label: Read This Page
